title: Integrity and attribution deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Integrity and attribution; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Integrity and attribution: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Adding a source link makes any copied solution acceptable, regardless of license or assessment rules.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Check both permission and attribution requirements, describe assistance accurately, and never represent another person's or tool's work as independent evidence of mastery.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Check both permission and attribution requirements, describe assistance accurately, and never represent another person's or tool's work as independent evidence of mastery.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Integrity and attribution instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Check both permission and attribution requirements, describe assistance accurately, and never represent another person's or tool's work as independent evidence of mastery.
Adding a source link makes any copied solution acceptable, regardless of license or assessment rules.
